label: High blood pressure
intro: Foods that support healthy blood pressure — often through dietary nitrates, potassium, or effects on blood-vessel function.

questions.text: Have you been told your blood pressure runs high?
Is your diet high in salt / processed food?

label: Kidney support (for drinkers)
intro: Foods and drinks that support the kidneys and liver — hydration, antioxidants, and compounds studied for protecting filtering tissue, useful if you drink alcohol regularly.

questions.text: How often do you drink alcohol?
Do you struggle to drink enough water?

label: Lung & airway support
intro: Foods rich in antioxidants and compounds studied for airway and lung function — of interest to ex-smokers or people in polluted air.

questions.text: Do you smoke, vape, or did you in the past?
Are you often around polluted or smoky air?

label: Hair loss & thinning
intro: Foods providing the protein, iron, zinc, biotin, and other nutrients hair follicles need — deficiencies in these are a common, reversible driver of shedding.

questions.text: Are you noticing more shedding or thinning than usual?
Do your nails chip or peel easily?

label: Low iron / anemia
intro: Iron-rich foods and the vitamin C pairings that boost absorption — for the tiredness, dizziness, and pallor of low iron.

questions.text: Do you feel dizzy or lightheaded when you stand up quickly?
Are you unusually tired or short of breath on light activity?
Do you look paler than usual, or crave ice/non-foods?

label: Immune support
intro: Foods high in vitamin C, zinc, and polyphenols studied for supporting immune defense.

questions.text: Do you catch colds or infections more than most people?

label: Skin health
intro: Foods supplying the vitamin C, carotenoids, and healthy fats the skin uses for collagen and barrier function.

questions.text: Are you working on skin health or complexion?

label: Gut & digestion
intro: Fiber-rich and fermented foods that feed the gut microbiome and support digestion.

questions.text: Do you deal with bloating, irregularity, or digestive discomfort?

label: Blood sugar & metabolism
intro: Foods with a gentle effect on blood sugar, plus compounds studied for insulin sensitivity.

questions.text: Have you been told your blood sugar or A1c runs high (prediabetes or diabetes)?
Do you get energy crashes or strong sugar cravings after meals?

label: Brain, focus & mood
intro: Foods supplying omega-3s, polyphenols, and micronutrients studied for cognition and mood.

label: Bone & joint health
intro: Foods rich in calcium, vitamin K, magnesium, and vitamin D for bone density and joints.

label: Eye & vision health
intro: Foods high in lutein, zeaxanthin, and vitamin A that support long-term vision.

label: Low vitamin D
intro: Vitamin D is hard to get from food and mostly made from sunlight, so it runs low in people with little sun exposure. These are among the few foods that actually contain it.

questions.text: Do you get little sunlight — mostly indoors, covered up, or dark winters?
Do you rarely eat oily fish?

label: Low vitamin B12
intro: Vitamin B12 is found almost only in animal foods, so vegans, vegetarians, and older adults are most at risk. Low B12 can cause fatigue, tingling, and brain fog.

questions.text: Do you eat little or no animal food (vegan or mostly vegetarian)?
Any unusual fatigue, tingling in hands/feet, or brain fog?

label: Low magnesium
intro: Many people fall short of magnesium, which the body needs for muscle and nerve function, blood pressure, and bone. Nuts, seeds, whole grains, and greens are the richest sources.

questions.text: Do you get muscle cramps, twitches, or restless legs?
Is your diet low in nuts, seeds, whole grains, and leafy greens?
